ingredients.name: Canal Cafe
ingredients.address: Canal Cafe, 1 Chome-9 Kagurazaka, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 162-0825
ingredients.entityContext: Canal Cafe by the Kagurazaka/Iidabashi waterfront is the scenic waterside café where Takuya meets his mother, serving as a setting for personal conversations against the backdrop of Tokyo's inner moat. Its relaxed terrace atmosphere mirrors the gentle tone the series sets for its quieter character moments.

ingredients.name: Glass Studio ZO
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒130-0001 Tokyo, Sumida City, Azumabashi, 3 Chome−4−5 大井ビル
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 9, Nao takes Rika to this Sumida glassblowing studio as a form of stress relief. The hands-on workshop helps Rika reconnect with herself before the emotionally charged reunion with her mother Ritsu. It's a turning-point scene that shows Rika rediscovering her own identity outside the fake engagement.

ingredients.name: FRESHNESS BURGER Kinshicho
ingredients.localName: フレッシュネスバーガー錦糸町
ingredients.address: ｶﾙﾑｶﾞｰﾃﾞﾝ錦糸町 1･2階, 4 Chome-29-1 Kotobashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Freshness Burger near Kinshicho station is among the casual lunch spots Takuya frequents, a grounded, everyday detail that roots the series firmly in the Kinshicho working-life setting the show is built around.

ingredients.name: Cosmo Clock 21
ingredients.address: よこはまコスモワールド, 2 Chome-8-1 Shinko, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The landmark Ferris wheel at Yokohama Cosmo World is where Takuya and Rika put on their wedding rings together for the first time, marking the moment their fake engagement begins to feel real. Fans of the series recognize this giant clock-wheel as the symbol of their relationship crossing the threshold from pretend to genuine.

ingredients.name: SMBC Fukagawa Branch
ingredients.localName: 三井住友銀行 深川支店
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-19-4 Kotobashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Haneda Airport Terminal 2
ingredients.localName: 羽田空港 第２ターミナル
ingredients.address: 3-chōme-4-2 Hanedakūkō, Ota City, Tokyo 144-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Haneda Airport Terminal 2 is the departure and arrival hub for the couple's key trips, most notably their journey to Takuya's Aso hometown in Episodes 4–5, where at the airport Takuya's grandmother intercepts them with freshly harvested melons. The terminal punctuates every trip the travel-agency protagonists take together.

ingredients.name: Iidabashi Station
ingredients.address: ４丁目-９-5 飯田橋, 千代田区 東京都 102-0072, Japan

ingredients.name: Tokyo Solamachi Tabe-Terrace
ingredients.localName: 東京ソラマチ タベテラス
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒131-0045 Tokyo, Sumida City, Oshiage, 1 Chome−1−2, 東京スカイツリータウン 東京ソラマチ ウエストヤード 3F
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Solamachi's dining terrace at the base of Tokyo Skytree appears as a lunch and after-work gathering spot for the office colleagues, consistent with its role as the main shopping and dining destination for Kinshicho/Oshiage area workers.

ingredients.name: Akebonoyu
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-17-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan

ingredients.name: Asahi Group Head Office Super Dry Hall
ingredients.localName: アサヒグループ本社 スーパードライホール
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-23-1 Azumabashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The unmistakable golden 'Asahi Flame' building beside the Sumida River is a visual landmark in establishing shots of the Asakusa/Sumida district where the series is set, appearing in skyline and riverside scenes.

ingredients.name: Kinshicho Station Platforms 1 and 2
ingredients.localName: 錦糸町駅1・2番線ホーム
ingredients.address: Sumida City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The JR Kinshicho platforms are the specific spot where Takuya and Rika's daily commute scenes play out, with catching-the-same-train moments that gradually shift from awkward coexistence to quiet companionship over the 365 days.

ingredients.name: Asakusa Batting Stadium
ingredients.localName: 浅草バッティングスタジアム
ingredients.type: バッティング センター
categories: バッティング センター
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-27-5 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan

ingredients.name: Hamura Station
ingredients.address: 1-chōme-7 Hanehigashi, Hamura, Tokyo 205-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hamura Station is the train access point for the Tamagawa Aqueduct area that Rika proposes for their Episode 10 date. It is the jumping-off point for the natural riverside walk where, in a decisive moment along the waterway, Takuya pulls Rika close and they share their first kiss.

ingredients.name: Madai Ramen Mengyo
ingredients.localName: 真鯛らーめん 麺魚 錦糸町本店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-8-8 Kotobashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Sumida Park Breeze Plaza
ingredients.localName: 墨田区立隅田公園 そよ風ひろば
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-3 Mukojima, Sumida City, Tokyo 131-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Sumida Park riverside plaza along the Sumida River provides a scenic outdoor backdrop near the characters' Kinshicho/Asakusa stomping grounds, used for leisurely walks and reflective scenes throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Kinki Nippon Tourist Yurakucho Store
ingredients.address: 駅構内地下 1階, 6 Chome-1-55 Uehonmachi, Tennoji Ward, Osaka, 543-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Waterfall of Oyokogawa Riverside Park
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒130-0011 Tokyo, Sumida City, Ishiwara, 4 Chome−28−10

ingredients.name: Nihondaigaku Tennis Center
ingredients.localName: 日本大学テニスコート
ingredients.address: グリーンピポット第３, 6 Chome-16-35 Saiwaicho, Tachikawa, Tokyo 190-0002, Japan

ingredients.name: Kumamoto Airport
ingredients.localName: 熊本空港（阿蘇くまもと空港）
ingredients.address: 1802-2 Oyatsu, Mashiki, Kamimashiki District, Kumamoto 861-2204, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kumamoto Airport (Aso Kumamoto Airport) is where Takuya's grandmother surprises the couple on arrival in Episodes 4–5, loading them into her truck along with a fresh haul of farm melons. It's the gateway to the Aso arc and the first time Rika sees Takuya entirely on his home turf.

ingredients.name: Sumida Park Fishing Pond
ingredients.localName: 墨田区立隅田公園 魚つり場
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-1 Mukojima, Sumida City, Tokyo 131-0033, Japan

ingredients.name: Imado Shrine
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-5-22 Imado, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0024, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Imado Shrine in Asakusa is one of Tokyo's most celebrated love and marriage shrines, famous for its paired maneki-neko cats said to bring romantic luck. Given the series' premise of two people staging a marriage, this shrine resonates as both an ironic and genuine destination for characters hoping their fake arrangement might become real.

ingredients.name: Kinshicho Station
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-14-6 Kotobashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kinshicho Station is the gateway to the JTC travel agency branch where Takuya and Rika work, and it anchors nearly every daily commute scene in the series. The station's platforms and surroundings form the backdrop for impromptu conversations between the two colleagues as their relationship slowly evolves.

ingredients.name: Café Knit
ingredients.localName: カフェ・喫茶 ニット
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-26-12 Kotobashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Asakusa Sta.
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-3 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Asakusa Station provides transit access between the Kinshicho/Sumida office neighborhood and the traditional Asakusa area, appearing as part of the characters' commute and errand geography throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Hamurakusabanakyuryo Natural Park
ingredients.localName: 羽村草花丘陵自然公園
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1 Tamagawa, Hamura, Tokyo 205-0024, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This hillside nature park near the Tamagawa Aqueduct in Hamura forms part of the scenic route Rika and Takuya walk during their Episode 10 date, the same outing where their feelings finally surface and they share a kiss. The lush, quiet trails reflect Rika's deliberate choice of an 'undramatic' setting that ends up being the most romantic moment of the series.

ingredients.name: Softbank Tokyo Solamachi
ingredients.localName: ソフトバンク東京ソラマチ
ingredients.address: 東京スカイツリータウン・ソラマチ １階, 1 Chome-1-2 Oshiage, Sumida City, Tokyo 131-0045, Japan

ingredients.name: Aso Volcano Museum
ingredients.address: 1930-1 Akamizu, Aso, Kumamoto 869-2232, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Aso Volcano Museum sits at the edge of the vast Mt. Aso caldera that dominates the landscape during Takuya's hometown arc in Episodes 4–5. The sweeping volcanic scenery establishes Aso as a place of raw natural scale, a stark contrast to the couple's urban Tokyo life, and is visible during the open-air farm scenes at Takuya's family homestead.

ingredients.name: Hikifune Station
ingredients.address: 2-chōme-26 Higashimukōjima, Sumida City, Tokyo 131-0032, Japan

ingredients.name: Kinshi Park
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-15-1 Kinshi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kinshi Park, the large green space closest to the JTC Kinshicho office, serves as a lunchtime and after-work retreat for Takuya and Rika. The park's quiet paths and open lawns provide a natural setting for some of their more candid conversations during work breaks.

ingredients.name: Honjo-Azumabashi
ingredients.address: 3-chōme-7 Azumabashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Koúme Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-３２ Mukojima, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Sumida Torifo Knee Hall Iriguchi Bus Stop

ingredients.address: 4-chōme-5 Kamezawa, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0014, Japan

ingredients.name: Hamura City Tourist Information Center
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-13-15 Hanehigashi, Hamura, Tokyo 205-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located near Hamura Station, this visitor center is part of the Hamura area that Rika researches and selects as the destination for their Episode 10 date, a practical, low-key choice that turns out to be the setting for the series' emotional turning point.

ingredients.name: Nippon Maru Memorial Park
ingredients.localName: 日本丸メモリアルパーク
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-1 Minatomirai, Nishi Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 220-0012, Japan

ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1 Minatomirai, Nishi Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 220-0012, Japan

ingredients.address: 2階, 4 Chome-27-14 Kotobashi, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0022, Japan

ingredients.address: 6 Chome-36-1 Saiwaicho, Tachikawa, Tokyo 190-0002, Japan

ingredients.address: 〒205-0021 Tokyo, Hamura, Kawasaki, 3 Chome−10−1 RetroBB

ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Kaminarimon gate and Nakamise shopping street of Asakusa appear as a recognizable Tokyo backdrop in the series, reflecting the Sumida/Asakusa neighborhood that surrounds the characters' daily lives near the JTC Kinshicho office.

ingredients.name: Super D'station Kinshicho
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒130-0022 Tokyo, Sumida City, Kotobashi, 4 Chome−25−5 鈴木ビル, 1•2•3F

ingredients.address: 1763-5 Fussa, Tokyo 197-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: A riverside green space along the historic Tamagawa Aqueduct in the Fussa/Hamura area, part of the same nature walk route Rika plans for the Episode 10 date. The waterway scenery here frames the episode's pivotal confession scene.

ingredients.name: 7-Eleven JR Kinshicho Station
ingredients.localName: セブン-イレブン ＪＲ錦糸町駅前店

categories: ショッピング モール
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-12 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The retro underground shopping arcade beneath Asakusa appears as part of the neighborhood texture surrounding the characters' Sumida-area lives, reflecting the old-Tokyo character of the district.

ingredients.name: Solamachi Hiroba
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒131-0045 Tokyo, Sumida City, Oshiage, 1 Chome−1 ソラマチ商店街 東京ソラマチ 1F, 西側出口前
ingredients.entityContext: The open plaza at the foot of Tokyo Skytree is a recurring outdoor meeting space for the characters, convenient to the Kinshicho branch office and used for casual encounters between colleagues.

ingredients.name: Ruins of horse watering hole
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒205-0014 Tokyo, Hamura, Hanehigashi, 2 Chome−16−14
ingredients.entityContext: One of the historical waypoints along the Tamagawa Aqueduct trail in Hamura that Rika and Takuya pass during their Episode 10 nature date. The old aqueduct infrastructure gives their walk a sense of time and place beyond the city, matching the episode's theme of the two finally seeing each other clearly.

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒187-0033 Tokyo, Kodaira, 1
